title: celtica gigantea
intro: Celtica gigantea, commonly known as Giant Celtis, is a plant species in the family Ulmaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it a popular choice in traditional medicine for treating various ailments such as fever, respiratory issues, and skin conditions. The therapeutic actions of Celtica gigantea are attributed to its bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, tannins, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to exhibit analgesic, antispasmodic, and anti-ulcerative activities. Furthermore, the plant has been used to treat digestive problems, and its extracts have been studied for their potential to inhibit the growth of certain pathogens, highlighting its significance in traditional medicine and potential applications in modern pharmacology.
side_effects: Celtica gigantea can have side effects if used improperly, such as dizziness, nausea, and headaches due to its high concentration of alkaloids. Prolonged use can lead to liver damage, kidney problems, and an increased risk of seizures in individuals with a history of epilepsy. In rare cases, excessive consumption of Celtica gigantea may cause hallucinations, rapid heartbeat, and respiratory distress. It is essential to consult a medical professional before using this plant for medicinal purposes to avoid these potential side effects.
